Activity 8: FluSight-Compliant Final Forecast

  • 1 Run The Workflow
  • 2 Load Outputs
  • 3 Inputs And Leading Signals
  • 4 Model Specification
  • 5 Candidate Ranking
  • 6 Horizon-Level Performance
  • 7 Interval Calibration
  • 8 Final Forecast
  • 9 FluSight Compliance Audit
  • 10 Strengths And Weaknesses
  • 11 Reproducibility Summary

This activity turns the best-performing forecast from the expanded modeling work into a FluSight-ready product. The workflow is deliberately conservative: it preserves the Activity 3 reference dates, horizons, target definition, and quantile format; compares ARIMA, Activity 5, Activity 7 XGBoost, Activity 7 XGBoost with leading indicators, ARIMAX, calibrated, and blended candidates with held-out weighted interval score (WIS); and validates the final submission files before they are written.

knitr::opts_chunk$set(message = FALSE, warning = TRUE, fig.width = 10, fig.height = 6)

find_project_dir <- function(start_dir = getwd()) {
  candidates <- unique(c(
    start_dir,
    file.path(start_dir, "USE-ME", "carolmoreira"),
    file.path(start_dir, ".."),
    file.path(start_dir, "..", ".."),
    file.path(start_dir, "..", "..", "..")
  ))
  candidates <- normalizePath(candidates, mustWork = FALSE)
  candidates <- candidates[file.exists(file.path(candidates, "output/scripts/08_flusight.R"))]
  if (!length(candidates)) stop("Could not locate USE-ME/carolmoreira.")
  candidates[1]
}

project_dir <- find_project_dir()
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = project_dir)
if (!isTRUE(getOption("knitr.in.progress"))) setwd(project_dir)
cat("Working directory:", project_dir, "\n")
## Working directory: /Users/carolmoreira/sismid/SISMID-2026-HumanAI/USE-ME/carolmoreira

1 Run The Workflow

The script below is the reproducible source of truth for Activity 8. It rebuilds the ARIMAX candidates, blends them with the Activity 5 ensemble and original ARIMA forecast, selects the best model by held-out mean WIS, and writes the final FluSight files.

source("output/scripts/08_flusight.R")
## Warning: package 'dplyr' was built under R version 4.4.3
## Warning: package 'forecast' was built under R version 4.4.3
## Season Start Week: 2025-09-28 
## Season End Week: 2026-05-17

## External regressors: NWSS WVAL lag 6 weeks; NSSP smoothed influenza lag 6 weeks.
## Final FluSight model: Activity 7 XGBoost 
## Final forecast: output/data/08_flusight/flusight_final_forecasts.csv 
## Submission files: output/data/08_flusight/final_flusight_submission 
## [val] quantiles non-decreasing: OK
## [val] all quantile levels present: OK
## [val] target dates correct: OK
## [diag] intervals widen with horizon: not required for selected direct/hybrid model
## [val] one file per reference date: OK
## # A tibble: 27 x 8
##    model      horizon n_forecasts   mae  rmse mean_wis coverage_95 mean_width_95

2 Load Outputs

required <- c("readr", "dplyr", "tidyr", "ggplot2", "knitr")
missing <- required[!vapply(required, requireNamespace, logical(1), quietly = TRUE)]
if (length(missing)) stop("Required R package(s) not installed: ", paste(missing, collapse = ", "))

library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(knitr)
truth_csv <- "output/data/01_cleaning/cleaned_flu_admissions.csv"
inventory_csv <- "output/data/08_flusight/upstream_data_stream_inventory.csv"
spec_csv <- "output/data/08_flusight/arimax_specification.csv"
comparison_csv <- "output/data/08_flusight/arimax_model_comparison_by_horizon.csv"
ranking_csv <- "output/data/08_flusight/flusight_model_ranking.csv"
final_forecast_csv <- "output/data/08_flusight/flusight_final_forecasts.csv"
submission_manifest_csv <- "output/data/08_flusight/final_flusight_submission_manifest.csv"
compliance_audit_csv <- "output/data/08_flusight/flusight_compliance_audit.csv"

stream_png <- "output/figures/08_flusight/surveillance_streams_overview.png"
lag_png <- "output/figures/08_flusight/lagged_covariate_relationships.png"
component_png <- "output/figures/08_flusight/blend_vs_component_medians.png"
ranking_png <- "output/figures/08_flusight/flusight_model_ranking.png"
final_forecast_png <- "output/figures/08_flusight/flusight_final_forecast_vs_observed.png"
metric_png <- "output/figures/08_flusight/arimax_metric_comparison.png"
calibration_png <- "output/figures/08_flusight/rolling_interval_calibration_multipliers.png"

expected <- c(truth_csv, inventory_csv, spec_csv, comparison_csv, ranking_csv,
              final_forecast_csv, submission_manifest_csv, compliance_audit_csv,
              stream_png, lag_png, component_png, ranking_png, final_forecast_png,
              metric_png, calibration_png)
if (!all(file.exists(expected))) {
  stop("Missing Activity 8 output(s): ", paste(expected[!file.exists(expected)], collapse = ", "))
}

3 Inputs And Leading Signals

The final candidate set uses the cleaned national influenza hospitalization target, the Activity 7 XGBoost forecasts, and two upstream national surveillance streams: NWSS wastewater viral activity level and NSSP smoothed influenza emergency-department visit percent. The external surveillance signals are lagged six weeks before ARIMAX and XGBoost-leading modeling, which makes them available before every 1-, 2-, and 3-week-ahead target date.

read_csv(inventory_csv, show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
  kable(caption = "Input stream inventory and model role.")
Input stream inventory and model role.
stream source_file rows date_start date_end model_use
Cleaned HRD admissions output/data/01_cleaning/cleaned_flu_admissions.csv 307 2020-08-08 2026-06-20 Forecast target
NWSS WVAL data/NWSSWVALNational.csv 104 2024-07-13 2026-07-04 External regressor, lagged 6 weeks
NSSP influenza ED visits data/NSSPNational.csv 197 2022-10-01 2026-07-04 External regressor, lagged 6 weeks
Historical truth iterations data/time-series-all-historical-iterations.csv 6805 2022-02-05 2026-06-06 Inventory only; real-time truth archive, not an exogenous predictor

4 Model Specification

The central ARIMAX candidate uses lagged NWSS WVAL, lagged NSSP influenza ED visits, deterministic MMWR timing features, lagged current-season cumulative admissions, and a lagged post-peak-decline flag. The Activity 7 XGBoost candidate uses admissions lags and seasonal harmonics, and the XGBoost-leading candidate adds lagged WVAL/NSSP. The post-peak ARIMAX feature is computed from the peak observed so far, then lagged, so it does not use the eventual full-season peak.

read_csv(spec_csv, show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
  kable(caption = "ARIMAX specification used inside the final candidate set.")
ARIMAX specification used inside the final candidate set.
model regressors regressor_lag_weeks leakage_rule reference_dates quantile_levels
ARIMAX: surveillance + seasonal NWSS National WVAL; NSSP smoothed influenza ED visit percentage; MMWR week sine/cosine; weeks since season start; lagged current-season cumulative admissions; lagged post-peak decline indicator 6 For target week t, use covariates from t - 42 days; horizon 3 uses covariates no later than 21 days before the reference date. 34 23

5 Candidate Ranking

The selected production forecast is the lowest mean WIS candidate across all scored reference dates and horizons. WIS is the primary ranking metric because it rewards both sharpness and calibration. MAE and RMSE are shown as supporting central-forecast diagnostics, while coverage and interval width describe uncertainty behavior.

ranking <- read_csv(ranking_csv, show_col_types = FALSE)
best_model <- ranking$model[1]
ranking %>%
  mutate(across(c(mean_wis, mae, rmse, coverage_95, mean_width_95), round, 3)) %>%
  kable(caption = "Overall model ranking. Lower WIS is better.")
## Warning: There was 1 warning in `mutate()`.
## i In argument: `across(...)`.
## Caused by warning:
## ! The `...` argument of `across()` is deprecated as of dplyr 1.1.0.
## Supply arguments directly to `.fns` through an anonymous function instead.
## 
##   # Previously
##   across(a:b, mean, na.rm = TRUE)
## 
##   # Now
##   across(a:b, \(x) mean(x, na.rm = TRUE))
Overall model ranking. Lower WIS is better.
model scored_forecasts mean_wis mae rmse coverage_95 mean_width_95
Activity 7 XGBoost 102 2187.141 3259.706 6424.846 0.882 14112.35
Blend: 50% XGBoost, 25% ARIMAX, 15% ensemble, 10% ARIMA 102 2393.696 3537.069 6352.136 0.922 14682.34
Activity 7 XGBoost + leading indicators 102 2768.881 4665.088 8379.926 0.941 26938.91
Blend: 50% ARIMAX, 25% ensemble, 25% ARIMA 102 3104.474 4116.961 7320.937 0.873 15254.14
ARIMAX: surveillance + seasonal 102 3199.393 4714.745 7072.458 0.892 18846.90
Calibrated blend 102 3209.510 4116.961 7320.937 0.892 22649.97
Calibrated ARIMAX 102 3215.370 4714.745 7072.458 0.912 22384.03
Activity 5 ensemble 102 3272.015 4098.559 7986.329 0.863 11643.84
Original ARIMA 102 3331.272 4154.706 8233.335 0.863 11678.82

The final model selected by held-out mean WIS is:

cat(best_model)
## Activity 7 XGBoost

6 Horizon-Level Performance

comparison <- read_csv(comparison_csv, show_col_types = FALSE)
comparison %>%
  mutate(across(c(mae, rmse, mean_wis, coverage_95, mean_width_95), round, 3)) %>%
  arrange(horizon, mean_wis) %>%
  kable(caption = "Model comparison by forecast horizon.")

7 Interval Calibration

Rolling interval calibration was evaluated as an explicit candidate rather than assumed to be better. It improved coverage for some horizons but increased interval width substantially. After Activity 7 was added, XGBoost-style direct quantile forecasts were also allowed into the final ranking; for those models, horizon-wise interval widening is reported diagnostically rather than treated as a hard stop because direct per-horizon quantile models are not guaranteed to widen monotonically.

8 Final Forecast

The final forecast keeps the required FluSight long format: one row per reference date, target, horizon, location, output type, quantile level, and forecast value.

final_fc <- read_csv(final_forecast_csv, show_col_types = FALSE)
final_fc %>%
  arrange(reference_date, horizon, output_type_id) %>%
  head(12) %>%
  kable(caption = "Preview of the final FluSight forecast file.")
Preview of the final FluSight forecast file.

9 FluSight Compliance Audit

read_csv(compliance_audit_csv, show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
  kable(caption = "Automated compliance checks applied before final export.")
Automated compliance checks applied before final export.
check status detail
canonical_column_order OK reference_date, target, horizon, target_end_date, location, output_type, output_type_id, value
date_columns_are_Date OK reference_date and target_end_date parsed as Date before writing
target_dates_equal_reference_plus_horizon OK validated for every row
full_23_quantile_ladder OK 0.01, 0.025, 0.05, 0.1, 0.15, 0.2, 0.25, 0.3, 0.35, 0.4, 0.45, 0.5, 0.55, 0.6, 0.65, 0.7, 0.75, 0.8, 0.85, 0.9, 0.95, 0.975, 0.99
nondecreasing_quantiles OK validated within every reference_date/horizon
nonnegative_integer_values OK validated by integer and lower-bound checks
horizons_are_1_2_3 OK exactly 1, 2, and 3
rows_per_reference_date OK 69 rows per reference date: 3 horizons x 23 quantiles
intervals_widen_with_horizon DIAGNOSTIC diagnostic only for selected direct/hybrid model; not a FluSight schema requirement
one_submission_file_per_reference_date OK 34 files written
selected_by_lowest_mean_wis OK Activity 7 XGBoost
causal_post_peak_feature OK post_peak_decline uses peak_seen_before, then a six-week lag

The output directory contains one validated file per reference date. Each file has 69 rows: 3 horizons times 23 quantiles.

10 Strengths And Weaknesses

model_notes <- tibble::tribble(
  ~model, ~strengths, ~weaknesses,
  "Original ARIMA",
  "Simple, transparent, fully compliant, and hard to overfit.",
  "Uses no wastewater, syndromic, or seasonal state signal; reacts after admissions move.",
  "Activity 5 ensemble",
  "Stable central forecasts and narrower intervals than ARIMAX-style models.",
  "Does not directly use leading surveillance streams.",
  "Activity 7 XGBoost",
  "Best overall WIS after Activity 7 is included; especially strong at two and three weeks ahead.",
  "Less interpretable than ARIMA/ARIMAX and direct horizon intervals do not always widen monotonically.",
  "Activity 7 XGBoost + leading indicators",
  "Uses the requested WVAL/NSSP signals in a leakage-safe direct quantile model.",
  "Improved coverage but widened intervals and worsened WIS in this evaluation window.",
  "ARIMAX: surveillance + seasonal",
  "Uses lagged upstream indicators and interpretable seasonal features.",
  "Central errors improved less consistently than the blend; intervals can be wide.",
  "Calibrated ARIMAX",
  "Uses only prior forecast errors to adjust uncertainty and improves coverage.",
  "Wider intervals can raise WIS when added coverage is not enough to compensate.",
  "Blend: 50% ARIMAX, 25% ensemble, 25% ARIMA",
  "Balances leading indicators with stable baseline behavior.",
  "Still inherits some missed coverage from component intervals.",
  "Blend: 50% XGBoost, 25% ARIMAX, 15% ensemble, 10% ARIMA",
  "Second-best overall WIS after adding Activity 7; strong coverage and best one-week WIS in this run.",
  "Did not beat raw XGBoost overall because the added components weakened longer-horizon sharpness.",
  "Calibrated blend",
  "Most conservative blend-style uncertainty and better coverage.",
  "Too wide for the WIS objective in this evaluation window."
)

model_notes %>%
  kable(caption = "Qualitative interpretation of each model version.")
Qualitative interpretation of each model version.
model strengths weaknesses
Original ARIMA Simple, transparent, fully compliant, and hard to overfit. Uses no wastewater, syndromic, or seasonal state signal; reacts after admissions move.
Activity 5 ensemble Stable central forecasts and narrower intervals than ARIMAX-style models. Does not directly use leading surveillance streams.
Activity 7 XGBoost Best overall WIS after Activity 7 is included; especially strong at two and three weeks ahead. Less interpretable than ARIMA/ARIMAX and direct horizon intervals do not always widen monotonically.
Activity 7 XGBoost + leading indicators Uses the requested WVAL/NSSP signals in a leakage-safe direct quantile model. Improved coverage but widened intervals and worsened WIS in this evaluation window.
ARIMAX: surveillance + seasonal Uses lagged upstream indicators and interpretable seasonal features. Central errors improved less consistently than the blend; intervals can be wide.
Calibrated ARIMAX Uses only prior forecast errors to adjust uncertainty and improves coverage. Wider intervals can raise WIS when added coverage is not enough to compensate.
Blend: 50% ARIMAX, 25% ensemble, 25% ARIMA Balances leading indicators with stable baseline behavior. Still inherits some missed coverage from component intervals.
Blend: 50% XGBoost, 25% ARIMAX, 15% ensemble, 10% ARIMA Second-best overall WIS after adding Activity 7; strong coverage and best one-week WIS in this run. Did not beat raw XGBoost overall because the added components weakened longer-horizon sharpness.
Calibrated blend Most conservative blend-style uncertainty and better coverage. Too wide for the WIS objective in this evaluation window.

11 Reproducibility Summary

cat("Final forecast CSV:", final_forecast_csv, "\n")
## Final forecast CSV: output/data/08_flusight/flusight_final_forecasts.csv
cat("Submission manifest:", submission_manifest_csv, "\n")

cat("Submission directory: output/data/08_flusight/final_flusight_submission\n")
## Submission directory: output/data/08_flusight/final_flusight_submission
cat("Selected model:", best_model, "\n")
## Selected model: Activity 7 XGBoost
cat("Reference dates:", dplyr::n_distinct(final_fc$reference_date), "\n")
## Reference dates: 34
cat("Rows in final forecast:", nrow(final_fc), "\n")
## Rows in final forecast: 2346
